description: All supply, drain, waste, and vent lines are roughed-in and pressure-tested behind open walls and floors, Baldwin County rough plumbing inspection is passed, and superintendent signs off before insulation and drywall can begin.

items.label: Current stamped plumbing plans on-site and plumber has confirmed layout against framed conditions
Plumbing permit card posted at job site prior to first inspection
Fixture rough-in dimensions cross-checked against final fixture selections (toilets, freestanding tubs, custom showers) before lines are set
Coordination with HVAC and electrical subs complete — no conflicts at shared joist bays, chase walls, or beam pockets
All slab penetrations (if applicable) confirmed sealed and sleeved per plan before plumber begins above-slab rough
All drain lines sized per IRC Table 709.1 — confirm 3" minimum on water closets, 2" minimum on lavs and tubs
Drain slope verified at 1/4" per foot for horizontal runs 3" and under; 1/8" per foot acceptable for 4" and larger — superintendent spot-checked with level
All vent stacks continuous to roof penetration, flashed, and no vent termination within 10 feet horizontal of operable window or HVAC intake
Wet-venting and air-admittance valve (AAV) locations, if used, confirmed code-compliant and approved by Baldwin County inspector
P-traps installed at every fixture rough-in; no S-traps present
DWV system air-tested at 5 PSI for 15 minutes with no pressure drop before inspection is called
Clean-outs installed at base of all stacks and at every 100-foot horizontal run; locations accessible per plan
Water service line sized and material confirmed (PEX-A, copper, or CPVC per spec) consistent with selections and Baldwin County water authority requirements
Hot and cold supply lines run to every fixture rough-in per plan; all lines labeled at manifold
PEX manifold (if home is home-run plumbed) secured, labeled by zone, and located in accessible mechanical space
Supply lines pressure-tested at 100 PSI for 30 minutes with no pressure drop before walls are closed
Minimum 1/2" supply to all lavatories and water closets; 3/4" maintained on trunk lines feeding multiple fixtures
All supply penetrations through top and bottom plates sleeved or grommeted to prevent abrasion; fire-blocking intact after penetration
Hose bib locations confirmed against exterior elevation plans; vacuum breakers specified for all exterior hose bibs
All pipes notched or bored through studs and joists within code limits — notches in non-load-bearing studs max 40% of width; bored holes no closer than 5/8" to edge without a nail plate
Steel nail plates installed at every location where pipe is within 1-1/4" of face of stud, joist, or plate — 100% of locations verified by superintendent walk
No structural members cut or over-notched; any field modifications to engineered lumber (LVL, TJI) approved in writing by structural engineer before proceeding
Pipe hangers and supports installed per IRC 305 — PEX supported every 32", copper every 6' horizontal and 10' vertical; no unsupported long runs
All floor joist bays penetrated by drain lines have blocking or approved backing reinstalled flush to maintain subfloor fastening schedule
Freestanding tub drain and supply rough-in locations confirmed against tub manufacturer's spec sheet — floor flange centered within 1/4" tolerance
Curbless shower linear drain rough-in confirmed with tile setter for final slope, drain brand, and flange height relative to finished floor elevation
Body spray, rain head, and handheld shower valve rough-ins confirmed against client's fixture selections and valve manufacturer's required rough-in depth
Pot-filler rough-in (if selected) located per kitchen cabinet elevation drawings — confirm height and horizontal offset with cabinet installer
Outdoor kitchen, pool bath, and/or irrigation rough-ins stubbed and capped at correct locations per site plan
Gas rough-in (if plumber is running gas) pressure-tested separately at 10 PSI for 15 minutes; all drops to appliance locations stubbed and capped
Baldwin County rough plumbing inspection scheduled — all DWV and supply lines visible and accessible at time of inspection
Baldwin County rough plumbing inspection passed — approved inspection card signed and on file before insulation or drywall is ordered
Superintendent walked entire rough-in with plumber after inspection: all caps tight, no open lines, no debris in open drain lines
Plumber's punch list from inspection (if any) completed and re-inspected before phase is closed
Photos taken of all wall and floor cavities showing pipe runs, nail plates, and support hangers — uploaded to project Drive folder before walls close
Superintendent and project manager sign off on Plumbing Rough phase in project management system; drywall sub cleared to schedule
